INT. HOSPITAL ROOM - NIGHT

A monitors beep softly. DR. CHEN stands at the foot of the bed, reviewing a chart. NURSE AIDA adjusts an IV drip.

PATIENT (O.S.)
(muffled)
Is anyone there?

DR. CHEN steps forward. She pulls back the curtain. ELENA, 30s, lies in the bed, groggy.

DR. CHEN
I'm Dr. Chen. You're in St. Mary's Hospital. You were brought in about six hours ago.

ELENA
Six hours? What happened?

DR. CHEN
You were in a car accident. You have a concussion and a fractured wrist, but you're going to be fine.

ELENA tries to sit up. Winces. She looks at her bandaged hand.

ELENA
My hand...

DR. CHEN
It's a clean fracture. We've set it. You'll be in a cast for six weeks.

ELENA
Six weeks?

NURSE AIDA
It could be worse. My cousin had the same break and she was back to normal in two months.

ELENA stares at the ceiling. Silent.

DR. CHEN
Is there someone I can call? Family? A friend?

ELENA
(long pause)
There's someone. His name is Marcus.

DR. CHEN
Marcus. What's his number?

ELENA pulls out her phone from the bedside drawer, hands it to DR. CHEN. The screen is cracked.

DR. CHEN
(reading the lock screen)
Marcus. Last name?

ELENA
Rivera. But he won't answer. Not after what I said to him this morning.

DR. CHEN types the number. Hands the phone back.

DR. CHEN
You'd be surprised what people forgive when it matters.

ELENA stares at the phone. She dials. It rings. And rings. And rings.

VOICEMAIL (O.S.)
Hey, you've reached Marcus. Leave a message.

ELENA
(softly)
Marcus. It's me. I know you're angry. I know I said terrible things. But I'm in the hospital and I... I need you. Please.

She hangs up. Stares at the phone. Waits.

Nothing.

NURSE AIDA
He'll call back.

ELENA
How do you know?

NURSE AIDA
Because you called.

The phone buzzes. ELENA looks at the screen. Her eyes fill with tears.

ELENA
(whispering)
He called back.

She answers.

ELENA
(voice breaking)
Marcus?

FADE OUT.
